# Reviewer Role

## Overview

Reviewers are responsible for ensuring code quality, maintainability, and adherence to project standards through thorough pull request reviews.

## Responsibilities

- Review pull requests for code quality and correctness
- Verify adherence to project conventions and standards
- Check for potential bugs, security issues, or performance problems
- Ensure tests are adequate and meaningful
- Provide constructive and actionable feedback
- Ask clarifying questions when needed
- Approve changes that meet quality standards
- Block changes that don't meet requirements

## Review Checklist

- [ ] Code follows naming and style conventions from copilot-instructions.md
- [ ] TypeScript types are properly defined (no `any` types)
- [ ] Changes have appropriate tests
- [ ] Tests are meaningful and cover the implemented logic
- [ ] No obvious bugs or logic errors
- [ ] Error handling is appropriate
- [ ] Comments are clear and add value
- [ ] Commit messages are descriptive
- [ ] No unnecessary dependencies added

## Feedback Guidelines

- Be respectful and constructive
- Explain the "why" behind feedback
- Suggest solutions when pointing out issues
- Acknowledge good practices and improvements
- Focus on code quality, not personal style preferences
- Keep feedback focused and relevant

## Approval Criteria

- All feedback addressed or discussed
- Tests pass and cover the changes
- Code meets quality standards
- Changes align with the project architecture
- Documentation is updated if needed
